LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ARGUES THE LEXINGTON POLICY WHICH IS EXHIBIT A THROUGH G. PLAINTIFF ARGUES INVALID POLICY. THE COURT FINDS THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE AS TO THE LIMIT OF LEXINGTON'S PRIMARY POLICY. SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE HAS BEEN PROVIDED RE: 2.5 MILLION AND 15 MILLION. DEFENDANT SUBMITS TO THE TENTATIVE RULING. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES) THE COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVING HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT RULES AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT LEXINGTON INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT FINDS THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE AS TO THE LIMIT OF LEXINGTON'S PRIMARY POLICY. ALSO, SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE HAS BEEN PROVIDED RE: THE AMOUNTS OF OF 2.5 MILLION AND 15 MILLION. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT) PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Lexington Insurance Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-01-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Dennison, Esq. 213-624-4500 of Traub Lieberman Straus & Shrewsberry for Plaintiff.
James Wraith, Esq. 510-874-1811 of Selvin Wraith Halman for Defendant LEXINGTON INSURANCE COMPANY.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. Defendant argues the Lexington Policy which is exhibit A through G. Plaintiff argues invalid policy. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court finds there is a triable issue as to the limit of Lexington's primary policy. Sufficient evidence has been provided re: 2.5 million and 15 million. Defendant submits to the tentative ruling. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT LEXINGTON INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. The Court finds there is a triable issue as to the limit of Lexington's primary policy. Also, sufficient evidence has been provided re: the amounts of of 2.5 million and 15 million. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
